steps.title: Find the center (pencil)
steps.description: Make sure you're in pencil mode. Lightly mark a small dot for the center of the starfish, then sketch five short guide lines radiating outward from that dot — like the spokes of a wheel. Space them roughly evenly.

steps.title: Mark the arm tips (pencil)
steps.description: Still in pencil, place a small dot at the end of each spoke for the tip of each arm. Don't aim for perfect symmetry — real starfish have arms of slightly different lengths.

steps.title: Block the silhouette (pencil)
steps.description: Sketch a loose outline connecting the tips, curving inward between each pair of arms to make the classic five-pointed star. The valleys between arms can be gentle dips, not sharp Vs.

steps.title: Switch to ink and trace the outline
steps.description: Switch to ink. Trace the star silhouette in one confident pass, letting your line wobble slightly — starfish aren't geometric. Let each arm taper gently toward its tip.

steps.title: Ink the texture (ink)
steps.description: Still in ink, add a scatter of small dots or short dashes along each arm to suggest the bumpy skin. Don't cover every inch — a handful on each arm reads as texture, and the eye fills in the rest.

steps.title: Clean up
steps.description: Switch to the eraser and remove the spokes and any other pencil guides. The starfish should feel a little lopsided — that's what makes it look real.
